text: The Septuagint Greek implies that the boy, when born, is to observe the same rules as have been enjoined on his mother; but the Hebrew text makes the angel simply repeat that Manue’s wife is to follow the prescriptions he has already laid down. The Latin may be taken in either way indifferently.

The Latin version is probably meant to read ‘presenting it before the Lord, who does marvels; meanwhile Manue and his wife looked on’. But it is possibly meant to be rendered as above, which would be more in keeping with the sense given by the Hebrew text.
